title: Syngonanthus inundatus
taxonomy: Syngonanthus inundatus is classified as a species within the family Xyridaceae. This family comprises plants commonly known as yellow-eyed grasses. Syngonanthus inundatus is specifically a part of the genus Syngonanthus, which includes a variety of species found in tropical and subtropical regions. The genus Syngonanthus is often associated with wetland environments. As part of this classification, Syngonanthus inundatus is a monocot, characterized by having one cotyledon during germination.

intro: Syngonanthus inundatus, also known as Swamp Syngonanthus, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Eriocaulaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Syngonanthus inundatus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Syngonanthus inundatus is often prepared as a tea or infusion, or dried and used as a powder for various herbal remedies.
